memberdef.argsstring: (std::shared_ptr< ProcessingSubTaskQueueChannel > queueChannel, std::shared_ptr< boost::asio::io_context > context, const std::string &localNodeId, std::function< void(const std::string &)> processingErrorSink, uint64_t delayBetweenProcessingMs=20)
memberdef.name: ProcessingSubTaskQueueManager

detaileddescription.para:
Construct an empty queue manager.
queueChannel
- Task processing channel.
context
- IO context to handle timers.
localNodeId
- Local processing node ID.
processingErrorSink
- Callback for processing errors.
delayBetweenProcessingMs
- Delay between processing cycles (ms).

Set a timeout for subtask processing
processingTimeout
- subtask processing timeout Once the timeout is exceeded the subtask is marked as expired.

Create a subtask queue by splitting the task to subtasks using the processing code
subTasks
- a list of subtasks that should be added to the queue in subtasks to allow a validation
false if not queue was created due to errors

Asynchronous getting of a subtask from the queue
onSubTaskGrabbedCallback
a callback that is called when a grabbed iosubtask is locked by the local node

Transfer the queue ownership to another processing node
nodeId
- processing node ID that the ownership should be transferred

Checks id the local processing node owns the queue
true is the local node owns the queue

Changes the local queue state with respect to passed queue snapshot The method should be called from a processing channel message handler
queue
received queue snapshot

Changes the local queue state with respect to passed queue request The method should be called from a processing channel message handler
request
is a request for the queue ownership transferring

Returns the current local queue snapshot
the queue snapshot

Mark a subtask as processed/unprocessed
subTaskIds
- a list of subtask which state should be changed
isProcessed
- new state
Check whether queue has been initialized to prevent nullptr access to the queue.

Checks if all subtask in the queue are processed
true if the queue is processed

Sink that gets subtask assignment events
subTaskQueueAssignmentEventSink
* lambda or function handling the event

Retrieves the current timestamp associated with the queue.
The current queue timestamp as a 64-bit unsigned integer.
Sets the maximum number of subtasks that can be owned at one time.

parameterdescription.para: The maximum number of subtasks that can be assigned to a single ownership instance.

detaileddescription.para:
Updates the local queue with a snapshot that have the most recent timestamp
queue
- the queue snapshot

Check if the queue has any current work to do
bool if there is work to be done
Updates m_queue_timestamp_ based on current ownership duration
Calculates the timeout for grabbing a subtask in milliseconds.

This method determines the remaining time until the current lock on the subtask expires in the queue's time base. If the lock is already expired, a minimal timeout value is returned. The calculated timeout is capped at a maximum threshold to ensure a reasonable upper limit.

simplesect.para: The timeout duration in milliseconds for grabbing a subtask.

detaileddescription.para:
Updates the list of unprocessed subtask indices and
if any unprocessed subtasks are available
